docs.title: Home Assistant — Documentation
docs.text:
This documentation site describes the Home Assistant instance stored in this repository.

Key locations in the repository:

  • configuration.yaml and the configuration/ folder — main Home Assistant configuration.
  • .agents/skills/ — local skills and guidance used for maintenance and automation.
  • Git hooks / git-ha-ppens integration — repository is synchronized with the running Home Assistant instance in your environment.

This site is generated with MkDocs and includes basic overviews of configuration and installed skills. Use the Docker image to serve locally (see the project root Dockerfile).

Overview of the repository configuration for Home Assistant.

Structure:

  • configuration.yaml — the primary configuration file. Large blocks have been split into configuration/ includes for readability.
  • configuration/ — contains extracted files such as homeassistant.yaml, plant.yaml, sensor.yaml, and google_assistant.yaml.

Notes:

  • When editing configuration files, validate YAML syntax and, where possible, test changes in a safe branch before applying to the running instance.
  • Sensitive files (service account JSON, credentials) are referenced using !include and should not be committed in plaintext.

docs.title: Home Areas
docs.text:
Based on your scripts and automations, the following areas are defined:

  • Huiskamer (Living Room)
  • Tuin (Garden/Outdoor)
  • Amber (Amber's room)
  • Slaapkamer (Bedroom)
  • Milou (Milou's room)
  • Badkamer (Bathroom)
  • Gang Boven (Upstairs Hallway)

docs.title: Lighting Devices
docs.text:
Your setup includes multiple smart lights controlled via scripts and automations:

  • Living Room Lights - Controlled by lampen_normaal script with adjustable brightness (45%)
  • Bedroom Lights - Controlled by lampen_boven_uit script
  • Garden/Outdoor Lights - Controlled by tuin_lampen_uit script with conditional logic
  • Amber's Lights - Part of the upper floor lighting group
  • Milou's Lights - Part of the upper floor lighting group
  • Various Brightness Scenes - Preset brightness levels (20%, 40%, 45%, 50%, 90%)

docs.title: Switches
docs.text:
• Amber Raket Switch - Smart switch with scheduled on/off
  • Turns ON at 06:55 (Amber raket aan)
  • Turns OFF at 08:15 (Amber raket uit)
  • Huiskamer Schakelaar (Living Room Switch) - Wall-mounted control
  • Triggers lighting scenes when toggled on/off
  • Shelly Button - Wireless button device
  • Toggles lights in living room and bedroom areas

docs.title: Covers (Blinds/Shutters)
docs.text:
• Zonnescherm (Sun Shield/Awning) - Motorized shade
  • Open/close automation available
  • Integrated with cover domain controls

docs.title: Foscam IP Camera
docs.text:
REST-based sensors from IP camera at 192.168.1.34:88:

  • Foscam Motion Sensor - Motion detection alarm
  • States: Disabled, None, Detected, Not Determined

  • Scan interval: 3 seconds

  • Foscam Sound Sensor - Audio detection alarm

  • States: Disabled, None, Detected, Not Determined
  • Scan interval: 3 seconds

docs.title: Appliances (Wasmachines)
docs.text:
Your laundry setup has two monitored washing machines with power-based start/finish detection:

  • AEG Wasmachine
  • Start detection: power above 500W for 5s
  • Finish detection: power below 1W for 5m
  • Running state helper: input_boolean.aeg_washing_machine_running

  • Notification + TTS when cycle is complete

  • Samsung Wasmachine

  • Start detection: power above 500W for 5s
  • Finish detection: power below 1W for 5m
  • Running state helper: input_boolean.samsung_washing_machine_running
  • Notification + TTS when cycle is complete

docs.title: Croton
docs.text:
• Moisture sensor (humidity)
  • Battery indicator
  • Temperature sensor
  • Thresholds:
  • Moisture: 15-60%
  • Battery: Minimum 5%
  • Temperature: 5-32°C

docs.title: Dieffenbachia
docs.text:
• Moisture sensor (soil moisture)
  • Battery indicator
  • Temperature sensor
  • Thresholds:
  • Moisture: 15-60%
  • Battery: Minimum 5%
  • Temperature: 12-32°C

docs.title: Automation Triggers
docs.text:
• Time-based - Scheduled on/off (e.g., Amber raket at 06:55)
  • Device triggers - Physical button presses (Shelly button, wall switches)
  • State-based - Conditional logic based on light/switch status
  • Manual - Direct control via Home Assistant UI

docs.title: Control Protocols
docs.text:
• ZHA - Zigbee Home Automation (for compatible devices)
  • Shelly - Direct WiFi control for Shelly devices
  • MQTT - MQTT-based devices and messaging
  • REST API - Foscam camera and custom devices
  • Cloud APIs - Google Assistant, Nest devices, Tuya

docs.title: Entity Registry
docs.text: Devices are assigned unique entity IDs for automation and scripting. Device references include: - Device UUID for reliable identification - Entity IDs for state tracking - Domain classification (light, switch, cover, etc.)

docs.title: Areas
docs.text: Areas help organize devices by physical location: - Each light/switch/cover is assigned to one or more areas - Area-based automation possible (e.g., turn off all lights in Huiskamer) - Used in scripts for bulk control (e.g., huiskamer_lampen_uit)

docs.title: Enabled Integrations
docs.text: Your Home Assistant instance has the following integrations configured:

docs.title: Core Integrations (via default_config)
docs.text:
The default_config component provides access to standard Home Assistant integrations including:

  • Homeassistant - Core Home Assistant configuration and control
  • Frontend - Web UI with custom themes
  • History - Historical state storage and graphs
  • Logbook - Event log visualization
  • System Monitor - System health and diagnostics
  • System Log - System event logging
  • Auth - User authentication and authorization
  • Config - Configuration management UI
  • Mobile App - Mobile device integration support

docs.title: Text-to-Speech (TTS)
docs.text: • Google Translate - Text-to-speech using Google Translate API

docs.title: HTTP Configuration
docs.text:
• HTTP - Web server with reverse proxy support
  • Trusted proxies: 172.30.33.0/24 (internal HAOS range), 192.168.1.136/32 (Talos NodeIP)
  • X-Forwarded-For header support enabled

docs.title: Connected Devices & Protocols
docs.text:
• Bluetooth - Bluetooth device discovery and connectivity
  • Prometheus - Metrics collection for monitoring
  • Media Source - Media library aggregation
  • ZHA - Zigbee Home Automation (via automations with ZHA device triggers)
  • Shelly - Shelly smart switches and relays (via automations)
  • MQTT - MQTT message broker integration (referenced in automations)

docs.title: Assistant & Voice
docs.text:
• Google Assistant - Google Home integration with state reporting
  • Exposed domains: switch, light, climate, scene, script, cover
  • Service account authentication enabled
  • Project ID: home-assistant-389312

docs.title: Plant Monitoring
docs.text: • Plant - Soil moisture, temperature, and battery monitoring for plants

docs.title: Local/Custom Integrations
docs.text:
• LocalTuya - Local control of Tuya smart devices
  • ESPHome - Integration with ESPHome-based custom devices
  • Sonos - Sonos speaker integration
  • UniFi - Ubiquiti UniFi network device control
  • Broadlink - Broadlink IR blaster control
  • Midea AC - Midea air conditioner control
  • Nest - Google Nest devices (thermostats, cameras)
  • Android TV Remote - Android TV remote control
  • Git HA-ppens - Git integration for Home Assistant (config sync)
  • Afvalbeheer - Dutch waste collection schedule
  • Buienradar - Dutch weather integration

docs.title: Specialized Features
docs.text:
• Device Tracker - Device location tracking
  • Image Upload - Image handling
  • Templates - Template sensors and helpers
  • Utility Meter - Energy consumption metering
  • Tag - NFC/RFID tag support
  • Event - Custom events and triggers
  • IPP - Internet Printing Protocol
  • WebRTC - WebRTC camera support
  • Go2RTC - RTSP stream support (noted in project)

docs.title: Automations & Scenes
docs.text:
• Scenes - Custom scene definitions
  • Automations - Automation rules (based on automations.yaml)
  • Scripts - Custom scripts for complex workflows

docs.title: Configuration Structure
docs.text:
Configuration is split across multiple files for maintainability:

  • configuration.yaml - Main config file with integrations and includes
  • configuration/homeassistant.yaml - Core HA settings, auth, external URL
  • configuration/plant.yaml - Plant monitoring configuration
  • configuration/sensor.yaml - REST sensors (Foscam camera)
  • configuration/google_assistant.yaml - Google Assistant settings
  • automations.yaml - Automation rules
  • scripts.yaml - Custom scripts
  • scenes.yaml - Scene definitions (currently empty)

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
• External URL: https://ha.bartvanderhel.nl
  • Auth Providers:
  • Trusted Networks (local network IPs)
  • Home Assistant Local (standard login)
  • Service Account: Google Cloud service account for Google Assistant

docs.title: Skills & Automation Support
docs.text:
This repository includes a collection of agent "skills" used to assist with developing, reviewing, and maintaining Home Assistant configuration.

Location: .agents/skills/

Installed examples:

  • home-assistant-best-practices — guidance for safe Home Assistant configuration and refactoring.
  • Superpowers collection — using-superpowers, systematic-debugging, test-driven-development, writing-plans, and more.

How to use:

  • Read a skill's SKILL.md to learn its workflows and prompts.
  • Use the skills locally to plan changes, run verification steps, and request reviews before merging.
